ddls:
CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant's gender category */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant's age group */,
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant's employment status */,
  "children_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant's children status */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the participant */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the participant */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the participant */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the participant (e.g., high school, college, graduate) */,
  "annual_income" TEXT /* Annual income of the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_participants_employment_id FOREIGN KEY ("employment_id") REFERENCES employment_statuses ("employment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_participants_children_id FOREIGN KEY ("children_id") REFERENCES children_status ("children_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_participants_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES gender_categories ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_participants_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey */,
  "survey_name" TEXT /* Name of the survey */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the survey */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey was created */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey starts */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey ends */,
  "total_questions" INTEGER /* Total number of questions in the survey */,
  "survey_type" TEXT /* Type of survey (e.g., demographic, behavioral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant providing the response */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey to which the response belongs */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* ID of the question being answered */,
  "answer" TEXT /* Answer provided by the participant */,
  "answer_date" TEXT /* Date when the answer was provided */,
  "answer_time" TEXT /* Time when the answer was provided */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_question_id FOREIGN KEY ("question_id") REFERENCES questions ("question_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey to which the question belongs */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the question */,
  "question_type" TEXT /* Type of question (e.g., multiple choice, open-ended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_questions_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "behaviors" (
  "behavior_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each behavior category */,
  "behavior_name" TEXT /* Name of the behavior */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the behavior */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the behavior was analyzed */,
  "analysis_result" TEXT /* Result of the behavior analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("behavior_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_statuses" (
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment status */,
  "employment_name" TEXT /* Name of the employment status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the employment status */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the employment status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the age group */,
  "min_age" INTEGER /* Minimum age of the age group */,
  "max_age" INTEGER /* Maximum age of the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gender_categories" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender category */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the gender category */,
  "gender_type" TEXT /* Type of gender category (e.g., male, female, non-binary) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "children_status" (
  "children_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each children status */,
  "children_status" TEXT /* Status indicating if the participant has children */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the children status */,
  "number_of_children" INTEGER /* Number of children the participant has */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("children_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant accessing the data */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey data was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the survey data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_survey_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the survey */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey being accessed */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read-only, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_survey_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_survey_access_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id")
)
